title: Resolve duplicated timestamp state between HistoryNode and Block variants
description:
There are two parallel sources of truth for "when did this block happen":

1. `HistoryNode.timestamp: SystemTime` set by `History::append` to `SystemTime::now()` at insertion time (src/history.rs:102, 151).
2. `Block::UserMessage { timestamp }`, `Block::ReasoningTrace { timestamp }`, `Block::ToolCall { timestamp }`, `Block::ToolResult { timestamp }`, `Block::AgentMessage { timestamp }` — each variant carries its own `timestamp` field (src/history.rs:76, 80, 85, 90, 95).

`History::append` never reads the Block-internal timestamp; the test helper `user_message` sets its own `SystemTime::now()` inside the Block independently. This will quietly drift: once Step 4 (Anthropic ARModel) starts producing Blocks, the wall-clock value inside a Block and the one on its HistoryNode will diverge in ways no caller has spec'd.

Required: pick ONE source of truth.

Recommended resolution (smallest diff): remove the `timestamp` field from each `Block` variant. `HistoryNode` already carries the canonical insertion timestamp and is the authoritative wrapper. Callers that need a timestamp use `HistoryNode::timestamp()`.

Alternate resolution: remove `HistoryNode.timestamp` and require every Block variant to carry its own. Less preferable because (a) the wrapping node is the natural place and (b) Block variants would still each duplicate the field.

Acceptance:
- Exactly one timestamp field remains per logical event.
- All call-sites and the 3 history tests compile and pass.
- cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo nextest run all clean.

Resolve before Step 4 lands so the Anthropic ARModel implementation doesn't bake in the duplication.

title: Remove design-narration doc-comments in src/model.rs
description:
src/model.rs contains module- and item-level doc-comments that narrate design rationale rather than provide information useful to future readers, violating STYLE_GUIDE.md §Comments ("Documentation for modules MUST not contain usage examples" and "Comments grow too verbose when coding agents describe their current goal or task").

Specific offenders:
1. Line 2 (module doc): `//! `Usage` is a tuple variant on `ModelStreamEvent` so it shares the struct shape with `MessageComplete { usage: Usage }` without duplication.` — narrates a design tradeoff that is obvious from the type definitions five lines below.
2. Line 52 (ARModel doc): `/// `system_prompt` is passed explicitly because the Agent owns it (see plan.md §"Agent configuration").` — narrates rationale and cross-references blueprint/plan.md, which is user-curated and may move.

Required changes:
- Reduce src/model.rs line 1-2 to just `//! Model traits and provider submodules.`
- Reduce src/model.rs line 52 to either remove the doc-comment on `ARModel` entirely or replace with a one-line statement about what `complete` does (not why its parameters are shaped the way they are).

Acceptance: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, and cargo nextest run all pass. No other code changes.

title: Coordinator: separate bead-state commits from code commits
description:
## Goal
Update `.claude/agents/coordinator.md` to require the Coordinator to commit \`.beads/issues.jsonl\` and \`.beads/last-touched\` state changes as **separate, isolated commits** from any code-bearing change.

## Background
During Phase 1, the Coordinator's pattern has been:
1. `br update <id> --status in_progress`
2. (this dirties `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/last-touched`)
3. Set up the working-copy description with `jj desc`
4. Delegate to the Coding Subagent
5. The Subagent commits — and its commit now also contains the bead state change

This co-mingles bead state with feature code in the same logical commit, making history harder to read and harder to revert cleanly. It also makes `jj split`/`git reset --hard` on a failed attempt accidentally revert the bead-state transition.

## Required change to `.claude/agents/coordinator.md`
Add a new subsection under "Coordinator workflow" titled **"Bead state commit cadence"** containing:

> When transitioning a bead between statuses (open -> in_progress, in_progress -> done, etc.), the resulting `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/last-touched` changes MUST be committed as a standalone commit, NOT folded into any code change. Cadence:
> 1. Run `br update <id> --status in_progress`
> 2. Immediately commit ONLY `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/last-touched` with message `Mark <id> in_progress`. Use `jj new -m "Mark <id> in_progress" .beads/`.
> 3. Then create a fresh empty commit for the Subagent to work in: `jj new -m "<task title>"`.
> 4. Delegate to the Coding Subagent.
> 5. After Judge passes, run `br update <id> --status done` and commit ONLY the bead state files with message `Mark <id> done`.
>
> Rationale: keeps task-tracking changes orthogonal to code changes; makes `jj abandon` / `git reset` on a failed attempt safe (it reverts the code without losing the in_progress marker, which the Coordinator can use to detect the retry).

## Files that may change
- `.claude/agents/coordinator.md`

## Files that must NOT change
- Any other agent file
- Any source code

## Verification
- The new subsection is present and well-formed
- `git diff` shows ONLY changes to `.claude/agents/coordinator.md`
- (No build verification needed; doc-only change)

## Out of scope
- Updating Coding Subagent or Judge Subagent docs (those agents do not manage bead state)
- Retroactively splitting past commits

title: Phase 1 Step 1: Scaffolding
description:
## Goal
Create the empty module skeleton and the Cargo.toml dependency set so subsequent Beads have a place to land code. No business logic in this Bead.

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` (authoritative for data model and runtime semantics)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Crate structure", §"Dependencies", §"Implementation plan / Step 1: Scaffolding"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md` (especially: `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, no `mod.rs`, no `unwrap`/`expect` in production)

## Required file layout (non-mod-rs)
```
src/lib.rs              # `pub mod harness; pub mod user; pub mod agent; pub mod history; pub mod model; pub mod messagebus;` + top-level `Error` enum stub
src/harness.rs
src/user.rs
src/agent.rs
src/history.rs
src/model.rs
src/model/anthropic.rs  # module declared via `pub mod anthropic;` in src/model.rs
src/messagebus.rs
src/bin/pristine.rs     # already exists; leave its contents alone for now
```

## Cargo.toml dependency block (replace the current `[dependencies]`)
Adopt the exact set listed in plan.md §Dependencies:
- tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
- reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["stream", "json"] }
- serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
- serde_json = "1"
- futures = "0.3"
- tokio-stream = "0.1"
- uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4"] }
- eventsource-stream = "0.2"
- clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
- anyhow = "1"

Preserve the existing `[package]` block and both `[[bin]]` entries (`pristine` and `1p`).

## Crate root
Add `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` at the top of `src/lib.rs`. The `pristine.rs` binary already has it; keep it.

## Top-level Error enum stub
In `src/lib.rs`, define `pub enum Error { /* variants added in later beads */ }` plus the module declarations. Do not yet wire submodule errors — that lands when those modules acquire real `Error` types.

A `#[non_exhaustive]` empty enum cannot be constructed and is fine as a placeholder. If clippy objects to an empty enum, prefer `pub enum Error {}` over adding a placeholder variant, but only if it lints cleanly.

## Module stubs
Each new `.rs` file may be empty (zero-byte) or contain only a doc comment line. They must compile.

## Stray editor file
There is a stale emacs autosave at `src/#agent.rs#` from a prior aborted attempt — DO NOT include it in your commit and do not modify it. Verify after `cargo check` that it has not been picked up (cargo ignores files starting with `#`).

## Definition of Done
- `cargo fmt --check` passes
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` passes (no warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` passes (there will be zero tests; nextest must still exit cleanly)
- No `STYLE_GUIDE.md` violations
- Commit `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/last-touched` is left to the Coordinator; do not commit beads state from this Bead.

## Commit policy
One atomic commit titled along the lines of `Scaffold Phase 1 module tree and dependencies`.

## Out of scope (do NOT do)
- Any actual implementation of History, Block, Agent, Model, Harness, MessageBus
- Editing `src/bin/pristine.rs` contents
- Removing `src/#agent.rs#`

title: Phase 1 Step 2: History and Block
description:
## Goal
Implement the persistent-history primitives: `NodeId`, the `Block` enum, and a `History` linked-list/stack built on `Arc<HistoryNode>`. This is pure data plumbing; no async, no model, no agent yet.

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §History (authoritative)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 2: History and Block" and §"`NodeId`"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md` (no `unwrap`/`expect` in production; newtypes over primitives; no `mod.rs`)

## Implement in `src/history.rs`
- `pub struct NodeId(uuid::Uuid)` — UUID newtype with `pub fn new() -> Self` (v4) and a `Display` impl. Derive `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash`.
- `pub struct AgentId(uuid::Uuid)` and `pub struct UserId(uuid::Uuid)` — same newtype shape as `NodeId`. (Step 6 will expose `UserId`/Owner via Harness; Step 7 will wire `AgentId` from `AgentBuilder`. Define both here so `Block` variants type-check.)
- `pub enum Block` with the variants per ARCHITECTURE.md §History:
  - `UserMessage { from: UserId, content: String, timestamp: std::time::SystemTime }`
  - `ReasoningTrace { content: String, timestamp: std::time::SystemTime }`
  - `ToolCall { name: String, arguments: serde_json::Value, timestamp: std::time::SystemTime }`
  - `ToolResult { name: String, result: serde_json::Value, timestamp: std::time::SystemTime }`
  - `AgentMessage { from: AgentId, content: String, timestamp: std::time::SystemTime }`
  - Derive `Clone, Debug`. Do NOT derive `PartialEq` (avoids painful trait bounds on `serde_json::Value`).
- `pub struct HistoryNode { id: NodeId, timestamp: std::time::SystemTime, block: Block, parent: Option<Arc<HistoryNode>> }`
  - All fields private; provide accessors `id()`, `timestamp()`, `block() -> &Block`, `parent() -> Option<&Arc<HistoryNode>>`.
- `pub struct History { head: Option<Arc<HistoryNode>> }`
  - `pub fn new() -> Self` (empty history)
  - `pub fn append(&mut self, block: Block) -> Arc<HistoryNode>` — creates a new node parented to `head`, advances head, returns the new node Arc.
  - `pub fn head(&self) -> Option<&Arc<HistoryNode>>`
  - `pub fn linearize(&self) -> Vec<Block>` — walks from head to root collecting `Block` clones, then reverses so the result is root-first/oldest-first.
- `pub enum Error { /* keep empty for now: future variants will land when ops can fail */ }` (or omit the enum entirely if clippy objects; the wider crate `Error` does not yet reference `history::Error`).

## Unit tests (in the same file under `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`)
- `append_advances_head_and_links_parent`: after two appends, the head's parent points to the first node.
- `linearize_returns_root_first`: append three blocks, assert `linearize()` returns them in append order.
- `fork_shares_prefix_via_arc`: construct one history, then clone it (or clone its head) and append different blocks to each branch; verify both branches' linearizations share the common prefix and the original `Arc` count proves sharing (`Arc::strong_count` on the shared node is > 1).

Use `unwrap()` freely inside tests (per STYLE_GUIDE.md: acceptable in tests).

## Wiring
- Declare `pub mod history;` in `src/lib.rs` (the Step 1 Bead may already have done this).
- Do NOT add `history::Error` to the crate-root `Error` enum unless it exists.

## Definition of Done
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo nextest run` all pass.
- No STYLE_GUIDE.md violations.

## Out of scope
- Persistence / `HistoryStore`
- Any async code
- `prepare_for_completion` shaping (Step 7 deals with linearization in context of an Agent)

title: Phase 1 Step 4: Anthropic ARModel
description:
## Goal
Implement `AnthropicModel` — the first concrete `ARModel`. Uses the Anthropic Messages API with `stream: true` over SSE.

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §Model (authoritative)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 4: Anthropic ARModel"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md` (no `unwrap`/`expect` in production; typed errors; private API types stay private)

## Implement in `src/model/anthropic.rs`
- `pub struct AnthropicModel { client: reqwest::Client, api_key: String, model_name: String, base_url: String }`
  - All fields private. The `base_url` defaults to `https://api.anthropic.com` and exists so tests can point at a mock server later (no test uses it in Phase 1, but the seam is cheap).
- `pub struct AnthropicModelBuilder { api_key: Option<String>, model_name: Option<String>, base_url: Option<String> }`
  - `pub fn new() -> Self`
  - `pub fn api_key(mut self, key: impl Into<String>) -> Self`
  - `pub fn model_name(mut self, name: impl Into<String>) -> Self`
  - `pub fn base_url(mut self, url: impl Into<String>) -> Self`
  - `pub fn build(self) -> Result<AnthropicModel, super::Error>` — returns `model::Error::Configuration(...)` if `api_key` or `model_name` is missing. Constructs the `reqwest::Client` here.
- `impl ARModel for AnthropicModel` — `complete(&self, system_prompt, messages)` returns a pinned boxed stream.

### Request shape (private types in this file only)
Define `#[derive(serde::Serialize)] struct AnthropicRequest<'a> { model: &'a str, max_tokens: u32, stream: bool, system: &'a str, messages: Vec<AnthropicMessage> }` and `#[derive(serde::Serialize)] struct AnthropicMessage { role: &'static str, content: String }`. Translate `Block` variants:
- `Block::UserMessage { content, .. }` → `role: "user"`, content cloned.
- `Block::AgentMessage { content, .. }` → `role: "assistant"`, content cloned.
- Other variants are skipped in Phase 1 (Anthropic Messages API has no slot for them yet; `ReasoningTrace`/`ToolCall`/`ToolResult` are reserved for later phases).

Hard-code `max_tokens = 1024` in Phase 1; document the choice in a single-line comment.

Required headers on every POST:
- `x-api-key: <api_key>`
- `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01`
- `content-type: application/json`

POST URL: `{base_url}/v1/messages`.

### Response handling
Use `eventsource-stream` to parse the SSE byte stream from `reqwest`'s streaming body. For each event, dispatch on the SSE `event:` name:
- `message_start` → parse `{"message": {"id": ..., "model": ..., "usage": {"input_tokens": ...}}}` → emit `ModelStreamEvent::MessageStart { message_id, model }` and an initial `ModelStreamEvent::Usage(Usage { input_tokens, output_tokens: 0 })`.
- `content_block_delta` → `{"delta": {"type": "text_delta", "text": "..."}}` → emit `ModelStreamEvent::ContentDelta { text }`.
- `content_block_stop` → emit `ModelStreamEvent::ContentComplete { text: accumulated_so_far }`. Accumulate text per content block; reset on `content_block_start`.
- `message_delta` → `{"usage": {"output_tokens": ...}}` → emit `ModelStreamEvent::Usage(...)` with the cumulative counts. Hold the latest seen usage for the final `MessageComplete`.
- `message_stop` → emit `ModelStreamEvent::MessageComplete { usage }` using the last cumulative usage.
- `ping`, `content_block_start` → no-ops (do not emit anything).
- Any deserialization failure → yield a `model::Error::Deserialization` once and end the stream.

If the HTTP status is non-2xx, the first item yielded from the stream is `Err(model::Error::Api { status, message: body })` and the stream ends. Do NOT panic.

Use `async_stream::try_stream!` if `async-stream` is added as a dep — but plan.md did not approve it. Prefer hand-rolling the stream with `futures::stream::unfold` or `tokio_stream`. Document any new dep request as a FAIL with reasoning rather than silently adding one.

## Implement `From<reqwest::Error> for model::Error`
In `src/model/anthropic.rs` (so `src/model.rs` stays reqwest-free):
- Map to `model::Error::Http(err.to_string())`.

Same trick for any `serde_json::Error` → `model::Error::Deserialization(err.to_string())`.

## Tests
- A unit test that `AnthropicModelBuilder::new().build()` returns `Err(Configuration)`.
- A unit test that `AnthropicModel: Send + Sync`.
- A unit test that builds a request body for a small `[Block::UserMessage]` and asserts the serialized JSON contains the expected `messages` shape (use `serde_json::to_value` and assert on specific fields).
- One `#[ignore]`-gated integration test in `tests/anthropic_live.rs` (NEW file, must declare `use pristine::...`) that:
  - Reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from env; if missing, `eprintln!("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set; skipping")` and `return` early (do not fail).
  - Builds an `AnthropicModel` with `model_name = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"`.
  - Sends a tiny `UserMessage` ("ping") and asserts at least one `ContentDelta` is received.
  - Marked `#[tokio::test]` `#[ignore]` so `cargo nextest run` skips it by default; a developer runs it with `cargo nextest run --ignored anthropic_live`.

## Definition of Done
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo nextest run` all pass.
- The live test is `#[ignore]`d and not run by default.
- No STYLE_GUIDE.md violations.

## Out of scope
- Tool use, vision, JSON mode, prompt caching
- `Block::ReasoningTrace` / `ToolCall` / `ToolResult` translation (they are skipped, not errored on)
- Mock-server tests (the seam is left via `base_url`; populating it is a later Bead)

title: Fix dual [[bin]] manifest warning in Cargo.toml
description:
## Goal
Eliminate the cargo build warning:

    warning: file `/home/nvader/projects/pristine/src/bin/pristine.rs` found to be present in multiple build targets:
      * `bin` target `1p`
      * `bin` target `pristine`

This warning is emitted at the manifest level (cargo, not rustc), so it is NOT caught by `-D warnings` and has been silently accruing. Multiple Coding Subagents flagged it during Phase 1.

## Context
`Cargo.toml` currently declares two `[[bin]]` targets pointing at the same `src/bin/pristine.rs` file. The intent is to ship the binary under two names (`pristine` and `1p`), but Cargo does not support multi-naming via duplicate bin targets.

## Required change
Pick ONE of these resolutions and document the choice in a one-line comment in `Cargo.toml`:

1. Single canonical bin + post-install alias: Keep only the `pristine` `[[bin]]`. Drop the `1p` entry. `1p` can be provided via a justfile recipe or a tiny shim binary if needed.

2. Dedicated shim bin: Keep `pristine` canonical, and create a NEW `src/bin/1p.rs` that is one line: `fn main() { pristine::main_impl() }` (requires factoring `main` into `lib.rs` as `pub fn main_impl()`). This is more invasive.

Recommendation: choose option 1 unless the user has previously requested otherwise. The blueprint references `1p` only as a convenience alias.

## Files that may change
- `Cargo.toml`
- (option 2 only) `src/lib.rs`, `src/bin/1p.rs`

## Files that must NOT change
- `src/bin/pristine.rs` (content is owned by Step 8 / bd-2y6)
- Any other `src/*.rs`

## Verification
- `cargo build 2>&1 | grep -i warning` is empty
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo nextest run` all pass
- `cargo build --release --bin pristine` succeeds

## Out of scope
- Any change to binary behavior
- Rewriting the binary (that belongs to bd-2y6)

title: Reconcile canonical home of UserId/AgentId across Step 6 and Step 7
description:
## Problem
Beads bd-jdi (Step 6) and bd-3gm (Step 7) prescribe asymmetric strategies for the canonical home of the UUID newtypes:

- bd-jdi instructs: MOVE `UserId` out of `src/history.rs` into `src/user.rs` and re-export from `src/history.rs` via `pub use crate::user::UserId;` (canonical home becomes `user.rs`).
- bd-3gm instructs: keep `AgentId` canonical in `src/history.rs` and re-export from `src/agent.rs` via `pub use crate::history::AgentId;` (canonical home stays `history.rs`).

These two policies disagree on direction. If both are implemented as written, the codebase will have `history.rs` as the canonical home for `AgentId` but a re-exporter for `UserId`, which is gratuitous asymmetry. It will also make `Block::UserMessage { from: UserId }` resolve through a re-export path while `Block::AgentMessage { from: AgentId }` resolves directly - additional cognitive load for no benefit.

## Constraint
Both `UserId` and `AgentId` are referenced from `Block` variants in `history.rs`. Moving either definition out of `history.rs` creates a circular-looking dependency (`history` <- `user`/`agent`) that, while compilable, inverts the natural layering: low-level data types depending on higher-level domain modules.

## Resolution
Choose ONE policy and apply it to both types. Recommended:

- Keep `UserId` and `AgentId` canonically in `src/history.rs` (they are tightly coupled to `Block` variants).
- Re-export from `src/user.rs` and `src/agent.rs` so the public API surface offers `pristine::user::UserId` and `pristine::agent::AgentId`.

This requires editing bd-jdi to drop the MOVE instruction and replace it with `pub use crate::history::UserId;` - mirroring what bd-3gm already says for `AgentId`. No code changes to existing files are needed; this is a Bead-text fix.

## Acceptance
- bd-jdi description is amended so its `src/user.rs` instructions read: `pub use crate::history::UserId;` (no MOVE, no inversion of canonical home).
- bd-3gm description is unchanged.
- This Bead is closed with a one-line note pointing at the amended bd-jdi.

## Why P1
Step 6 and Step 7 will likely run in close succession or in parallel; landing them with conflicting policies will require a follow-up cleanup commit. Fixing the Bead text now is cheap.

title: Phase 1 Step 8: Binary
description:
## Goal
Wire the `1p`/`pristine` binary to: parse CLI args with Clap; read `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from env; construct a Harness with one `AnthropicModel` registered under `ModelRole::Default`; register one Agent with a hard-coded `system_prompt`; start the Harness; subscribe to its outbound event stream; send TWO sequential `UserMessage`s from the Owner; print `TokenDelta` text to stdout as it arrives; print usage on `RunComplete`; then `shutdown()` and `await` the Harness future cleanly.

The deliverable is the end-to-end demo from `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Initial Build" and plan.md §"Step 8: Binary".

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Initial Build" (authoritative for the end-to-end shape)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 8: Binary", §"Data flow", §"CLI surface"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`

## Pre-conditions
By the time this Bead runs, `Cargo.toml` should declare exactly ONE `[[bin]]` target named `pristine` pointing at `src/bin/pristine.rs` (bd-23q resolves the duplicate-target warning by removing the `1p` `[[bin]]` entry). This Bead RE-INTRODUCES the `1p` alias as a separate thin shim binary (see §"`1p` shim" below) so the user's success criterion (`1p` binary) is met without re-introducing the duplicate-target warning.

## Implement in `src/bin/pristine.rs` (full replacement)
- Keep `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Use `clap` derive.

### Clap surface
```
pristine run "first message"
```
Two-message demo. The first message is the positional CLI argument. The second message is hard-coded inside the binary as:

```
const SECOND_MESSAGE: &str = "Write me a poem of what it is like to be you, Pristine";
```

Per ARCHITECTURE.md §"Initial Build".

If no subcommand is given, default to `run`. Define one Clap subcommand `Run { message: String, #[arg(long, default_value = "claude-sonnet-4-6")] model: String }`. Document the `model` flag as the Anthropic model identifier.

### System prompt (hard-coded constant)
```
const SYSTEM_PROMPT: &str = "You are the Pristine agent. You have an identity that is uniquely yours!";
```

### Top-level error reporting
`#[tokio::main(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)]` and `async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()>`. Convert crate errors with `?`. The `worker_threads = 2` floor is required to avoid deadlocks between the streaming HTTP body and the Agent loop.

### Stdout streaming loop
```
let mut rx = harness.subscribe(agent_id)?;
harness.send_to_agent(agent_id, owner_id, first_message)?;

let mut runs_completed = 0;
let mut second_sent = false;

while runs_completed < 2 {
    match rx.recv().await {
        Ok(AgentEvent::TokenDelta { text }) => {
            use std::io::Write;
            print!("{text}");
            std::io::stdout().flush()?;
        }
        Ok(AgentEvent::RunComplete { usage }) => {
            println!();
            eprintln!("[usage: input_tokens={} output_tokens={}]", usage.input_tokens, usage.output_tokens);
            runs_completed += 1;
            if !second_sent {
                harness.send_to_agent(agent_id, owner_id, SECOND_MESSAGE.into())?;
                second_sent = true;
            }
        }
        Ok(AgentEvent::BlockComplete { .. }) => {}
        Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::RecvError::Lagged(_)) => continue,
        Err(tokio::sync::broadcast::error::RecvError::Closed) => break,
    }
}

harness.shutdown();
harness.run_until_shutdown().await?;
Ok(())
```

### Env var handling
- Read `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` via `std::env::var`. If missing or empty, return `Err(anyhow::anyhow!("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set"))`.

### Construction
Use the Harness/Agent builders:
1. Build the `AnthropicModel` via `AnthropicModelBuilder::new().api_key(...).model_name(args.model.clone()).build()?`.
2. `HarnessBuilder::new().add_model(ModelId::new("anthropic-default"), Arc::new(model)).add_agent(PendingAgent { id: AgentId::new(), system_prompt: SYSTEM_PROMPT.into(), model_id: ModelId::new("anthropic-default") }).build()?`.
3. Capture the AgentId BEFORE building (so the binary knows what to subscribe to). Pattern: construct `let agent_id = AgentId::new();` first, pass it into `PendingAgent`, then call `build`.
4. `harness.start()?;`
5. Subscribe BEFORE sending: `let mut rx = harness.subscribe(agent_id)?;` then `harness.send_to_agent(agent_id, harness.owner_id(), args.message)?;`

## `1p` shim — implement in NEW file `src/bin/1p.rs`
```rust
#![forbid(unsafe_code)]
// Thin alias binary so users can invoke `1p`. The real implementation is in pristine::run.
fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    pristine::run()
}
```

To support this, factor the body of `main` from `src/bin/pristine.rs` into a `pub fn run() -> anyhow::Result<()>` exported from the library at `src/lib.rs` (or via a new `src/bin_entry.rs` module). The factoring:
- Move the `#[tokio::main]`-wrapped logic into a synchronous wrapper that constructs the Tokio runtime explicitly:
  ```rust
  pub fn run() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
      let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()
          .worker_threads(2)
          .enable_all()
          .build()?;
      rt.block_on(async { run_async().await })
  }
  async fn run_async() -> anyhow::Result<()> { /* the real logic */ }
  ```
- Both `src/bin/pristine.rs` and `src/bin/1p.rs` then have one-line `fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> { pristine::run() }` bodies.

Alternative simpler approach: keep `src/bin/pristine.rs` with `#[tokio::main]` and full logic; `src/bin/1p.rs` is one line: `fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> { pristine::run() }`. Implement `pub fn run()` in `lib.rs` as the runtime-bootstrapping wrapper described above. `src/bin/pristine.rs` should also call `pristine::run()` to avoid code duplication.

Recommendation: **the second approach** (both bins call `pristine::run()` from lib.rs; both bin files are one-line `fn main()`).

## Cargo.toml change
- Add a SECOND `[[bin]]` entry:
  ```
  [[bin]]
  name = "1p"
  path = "src/bin/1p.rs"
  ```
  Distinct files mean no duplicate-target warning.

The existing single `[[bin]]` entry (`pristine` → `src/bin/pristine.rs`, post bd-23q) is preserved.

## Tests
- No tests in this Bead. The behavior is interactive and live-API-dependent. End-to-end validation is the live `cargo run` invocation by the Coordinator after Judge passes.
- Optional but encouraged: a compile-only smoke test in `tests/binary_smoke.rs` (no API call) that invokes a CLAP `--help` rendering via `pristine::cli::parse_for_test(...)` — only if it fits naturally. NOT REQUIRED.

## Manual verification
After Judge passes, the Coordinator runs:
```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=$(cat /home/nvader/projects/pristine/.env | grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | cut -d= -f2) \
  cargo run --bin 1p -- run "Introduce yourself to me, Pristine"
```
and confirms two sequential streaming responses print to stdout.

## Definition of Done
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` — 25 prior tests pass + 1 ignored
- `cargo build --release` — succeeds
- `cargo build` — emits NO duplicate-target warning
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`
- No STYLE_GUIDE.md violations

## Out of scope
- Configuration files
- Multi-agent demos
- Tool use

title: Resolve placeholder `pub enum Error {}` in src/lib.rs
description:
src/lib.rs:10 declares `pub enum Error {}` — an empty, uninhabited public enum with no constructors, no `Display`/`Error` impls, and no callers. It is dead surface area.

Two acceptable resolutions:
1. Remove the line entirely. Crate-level error consolidation can be designed when (or if) it is actually needed; right now each module that needs an Error type defines its own (e.g. `crate::model::Error`).
2. Replace it with a real top-level Error enum that wraps the module-level errors (e.g. `Model(crate::model::Error)`), with `From` impls and `Display` / `std::error::Error` implementations.

Recommended: option 1. The cost of premature consolidation is higher than the cost of adding it later. Steps 4-8 will reveal the actual shape needed.

Acceptance: src/lib.rs is clean of dead types. cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo nextest run all pass.

title: Clean up emacs auto-save files and tighten .gitignore
description:
## Goal
Two related issues:

1. There is a stray emacs auto-save file at `src/#agent.rs#` that survived from an earlier editing session. It is currently tracked by jj/git working copy and pollutes \`ls src/\`.
2. `.gitignore` covers `*~` (backup files) but does NOT cover emacs auto-save (`#*#`) or lock (`.#*`) patterns. Other agents have left stray `ARCHITECTURE.md~` and `README.md~` files in past commits that should also be cleaned.

## Required change
1. Remove the stray file: `rm src/#agent.rs#`. Use `jj` if needed; the file is not part of any module.
2. Add to `.gitignore`:
   - `\#*\#` (emacs auto-save)
   - `.\#*` (emacs lock files)
3. Remove already-tracked tilde files: `ARCHITECTURE.md~`, `README.md~`, `justfile~` (and any others surfaced by `ls *~ **/*~`).

## Files that may change
- `.gitignore`
- Deletion-only: `src/#agent.rs#`, `ARCHITECTURE.md~`, `README.md~`, `justfile~`

## Files that must NOT change
- Any `*.rs` source file content
- Any `.md` source content (only the trailing-tilde dupes are deleted)

## Verification
- `ls src/ | grep -E '^#|~$'` is empty
- `ls *~` returns nothing (or just an error)
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo nextest run` all pass

## Out of scope
- Any code change

title: Phase 1 Step 7: Agent event loop
Fix silent hang when Agent dies with error
description:
## Goal
Two coupled fixes that both touch `src/lib.rs`. Land them together.

**Fix A — Silent hang on agent error.** The binary hangs silently when an Agent task dies (e.g., the Anthropic API returns 401 with a bad key). Subscribers cannot detect this because broadcast `Sender` clones in `Harness` and `InMemoryMessageBus` keep the channel open after the Agent task exits. Resolution: extend `AgentEvent` with an `Error { message: String }` variant; the Harness emits it from its per-agent spawn task when `agent.run()` returns `Err`; the binary's streaming loop treats `AgentEvent::Error` as terminal and returns `anyhow::Err`.

**Fix B — CLI surface cleanup.** The current binary accepts `1p run <message>`. Per `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Initial Build" the two Owner messages are both hard-coded (`"Introduce yourself to me, Pristine"` and `"Write me a poem of what it is like to be you, Pristine"`). Remove the positional `message` arg from the `Run` subcommand. The `--model` flag stays.

## Reference
- `src/messagebus.rs` — `AgentEvent` enum
- `src/harness.rs` — `Harness::start` spawn body
- `src/lib.rs` — `run_async`, Clap parser, constants

## Required changes

### 1. `src/messagebus.rs`
Extend the enum:
```
pub enum AgentEvent {
    TokenDelta { text: String },
    BlockComplete { block: std::sync::Arc<crate::history::HistoryNode> },
    RunComplete { usage: crate::model::Usage },
    Error { message: String },
}
```
No other change. The `Error` variant carries a `Display`-formatted error message; the Harness constructs it directly. No `From` impls.

Add ONE test in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`:
- `subscribe_outbound_sees_published_error_events` — register an agent; subscribe; via the bus's outbound `Sender`, publish `AgentEvent::Error { message: "boom".into() }`; within `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), ...)`, assert the subscriber receives it and the message equals `"boom"`.

### 2. `src/harness.rs`
Modify ONLY the spawn body inside `Harness::start`. Pseudocode:
```rust
let outbound_for_emit = outbound.clone();
let cancel_for_task = self.cancel.clone();
let agent = AgentBuilder::new()
    .id(spec.id)
    .system_prompt(spec.system_prompt.clone())
    .model(ModelRole::Default, model.clone())
    .build(inbound, outbound)?;
let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
    let res = tokio::select! {
        _ = cancel_for_task.cancelled() => Ok::<(), Error>(()),
        res = agent.run() => res.map_err(Error::from),
    };
    if let Err(ref e) = res {
        let _ = outbound_for_emit.send(AgentEvent::Error { message: e.to_string() });
        cancel_for_task.cancel();
    }
    res
});
```
Notes:
- The `cancel` clone may already exist in scope; reuse if so.
- The `let _ = ...` discards send failure (no subscribers = best-effort, per the broadcast-is-lossy convention).
- The post-emit `cancel.cancel()` ensures `run_until_shutdown` does not stall in multi-Agent futures.

Add ONE new test:
- `harness_emits_error_event_when_agent_dies` — build a Harness with one Agent whose `StubArModel` yields a single `Err(model::Error::Api { status: 500, message: "boom".into() })`. `start()`, subscribe, `send_to_agent` a `UserMessage`. Within `timeout(Duration::from_secs(2), ...)`, assert the subscriber receives an `AgentEvent::Error { message }` where `message.contains("500")` AND `message.contains("boom")` (the agent::Error's Display chain should include the model error's Display). Then `harness.shutdown()` (idempotent) and `harness.run_until_shutdown().await`; the latter must NOT hang.

A new test-local `StubArModel` will need to support yielding a non-empty error stream (the existing Step 6 stub returns `stream::empty()`). Either parameterize it or add a second helper in the same `#[cfg(test)]` module.

The existing four Step 6 tests should continue to pass without modification. If they break because of the new cancel-on-error behavior, adjust the failing test minimally; do NOT broadly refactor.

### 3. `src/lib.rs` — `run_async` streaming loop AND CLI surface

**CLI surface change (Fix B):**
The `Run` subcommand currently has fields `{ message: String, #[arg(long, default_value = "claude-sonnet-4-6")] model: String }`. **Remove the `message` field.** Final shape:
```rust
#[derive(Clap subcommand)]
enum Cmd {
    Run {
        #[arg(long, default_value = "claude-sonnet-4-6")]
        model: String,
    },
}
```
The first message is now a hard-coded constant alongside `SECOND_MESSAGE`:
```rust
const FIRST_MESSAGE: &str = "Introduce yourself to me, Pristine";
const SECOND_MESSAGE: &str = "Write me a poem of what it is like to be you, Pristine";
```
Inside `run_async`, after subscribing, call:
```rust
harness.send_to_agent(agent_id, owner_id, FIRST_MESSAGE.into())?;
```
Inside the `RunComplete` arm, after `runs_completed == 1`, send `SECOND_MESSAGE` instead of the old `args.message`-derived value.

**Streaming-loop change (Fix A):**
Add a new match arm BEFORE the `Ok(AgentEvent::BlockComplete { .. }) => {}` arm:
```rust
Ok(AgentEvent::Error { message }) => {
    return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("agent error: {message}"));
}
```

### 4. Style
- No banner comments.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- No `plan.md`/`bd-` references in source comments.

## Files you MAY modify
- `src/messagebus.rs`
- `src/harness.rs`
- `src/lib.rs`

## Files you may NOT touch
- `src/agent.rs`, `src/model.rs`, `src/model/anthropic.rs`, `src/history.rs`, `src/user.rs`
- `src/bin/pristine.rs`, `src/bin/1p.rs` (these one-line shims do not change)
- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock` (no new deps; clap derive already covers the simplified subcommand)
- `.beads/`, documents, `tests/`

## Verification
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` — expect 27 tests pass (25 prior + 2 new: one in messagebus + one in harness) + 1 skipped
- `cargo build` — zero warnings
- `cargo run --bin 1p -- run --help` — Clap help should show ONLY the `--model` flag and NO positional `message` argument.
- Manual smoke test (Coordinator runs): `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=fakedeadbeef cargo run --bin 1p -- run` exits within ~10 seconds with a non-zero exit code and an `agent error: ...` message on stderr (do NOT hang).

## Out of scope
- Retry/recovery semantics
- Multi-Agent error handling beyond the cancel-on-error signal
- Changing broadcast channel capacity

title: Phase 1 Step 3: AR/DL Model traits
description:
## Goal
Define the model abstraction layer: `ARModel` trait (streaming completion), `DLModel` placeholder, `ModelStreamEvent`, `Usage`, `ModelRole`, and `model::Error`. No concrete implementation yet — Anthropic lands in Step 4.

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §Model (authoritative)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 3: AR/DL Model traits", §"`ModelStreamEvent`", §"`ModelRole` enum"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`

## Implement in `src/model.rs`
- `pub enum ModelRole { Default }` — derive `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash`. Plan.md commits to a single `Default` variant in Phase 1.
- `pub struct Usage { pub input_tokens: u32, pub output_tokens: u32 }` — derive `Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq`.
- `pub enum ModelStreamEvent` — variants exactly as in plan.md §"`ModelStreamEvent`":
  - `MessageStart { message_id: String, model: String }`
  - `ContentDelta { text: String }`
  - `ContentComplete { text: String }`
  - `Usage(Usage)` — keep this newtype-style variant so the `Usage` event from plan.md round-trips cleanly. (Plan.md lists both `MessageComplete { usage: Usage }` and `Usage { input_tokens, output_tokens }`; collapse the bare `Usage { … }` into `Usage(Usage)` and keep `MessageComplete { usage: Usage }` as a separate variant. Document the rationale in a single-line module comment.)
  - `MessageComplete { usage: Usage }`
  - `Error { message: String }`
  - Derive `Clone, Debug`.
- `pub enum Error` — typed model errors. Variants:
  - `Http(String)` (will carry `reqwest::Error`-derived messages in Step 4 without exposing reqwest types here — store `String` and convert via `From<reqwest::Error>` in `model/anthropic.rs`).
  - `Deserialization(String)` (likewise for SSE / JSON failures).
  - `Api { status: u16, message: String }` (HTTP non-2xx with a body).
  - `Configuration(String)` (missing API key, malformed builder input).
  - Derive `Debug` and implement `std::fmt::Display` and `std::error::Error` by hand (no `thiserror`; that dep is not approved).
- `ARModel` trait — signature (lifetime parameter shown literally; reproduce verbatim):

```
pub trait ARModel: Send + Sync {
    fn complete<'a>(
        &'a self,
        system_prompt: &'a str,
        messages: &'a [crate::history::Block],
    ) -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn futures::Stream<Item = Result<ModelStreamEvent, Error>> + Send + 'a>>;
}
```

The `system_prompt` parameter is added relative to plan.md §"Step 3" because the Agent owns the system prompt (plan.md §"`Agent` configuration") and the model needs it for the Anthropic call. Justified deviation; document with a one-line comment on the trait.
- `DLModel` trait — empty marker trait per plan.md §"`ARModel` and `DLModel`":

```
pub trait DLModel: Send + Sync {}
```

## Module declarations
- `src/model.rs` declares `pub mod anthropic;` (Step 4 fills it; for now `src/model/anthropic.rs` must exist as an empty file — Step 1 should have created it).
- `src/lib.rs` already declares `pub mod model;` (Step 1 work).

## Unit tests
- One test asserting `ModelStreamEvent: Send + Sync` (`fn assert_send_sync<T: Send + Sync>() {}; assert_send_sync::<ModelStreamEvent>();`).
- One test asserting `Error: std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static`.

## Definition of Done
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo nextest run` all pass.
- No STYLE_GUIDE.md violations.

## Out of scope
- Any HTTP / SSE / Anthropic-specific code (Step 4)
- Wiring `model::Error` into the crate-root `Error` enum (deferred to a later Bead when all submodule errors land together)

title: Phase 1 Step 5: MessageBus and AgentEvent
description:
## Goal
Define the `AgentEvent` outbound wire type and the `MessageBus` trait, plus an `InMemoryMessageBus` implementation that routes one Agent's completed Blocks to another Agent's inbound stream. Phase 1 has a single Agent so routing is trivial; the trait shape must accommodate fan-out and (eventually) cross-process implementations.

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §MessageBus, §Agent (authoritative)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 5: MessageBus and AgentEvent", §"Outbound channel", §"Inbound channel"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`

## Implement in `src/messagebus.rs`
- `pub enum AgentEvent`:
  - `TokenDelta { text: String }`
  - `BlockComplete { block: std::sync::Arc<crate::history::HistoryNode> }`
  - `RunComplete { usage: crate::model::Usage }`
  - Derive `Clone, Debug`.
- `pub enum Error`:
  - `UnknownAgent(crate::history::AgentId)`
  - `Closed` (channel dropped before send/recv could complete)
  - Manual `Display` and `std::error::Error`.
- `pub trait MessageBus: Send + Sync`:
  ```
  fn register(
      &self,
      agent_id: crate::history::AgentId,
  ) -> Result<(
      tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<AgentEvent>,
      tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<crate::history::Block>,
  ), Error>;

  fn outbound(
      &self,
      agent_id: crate::history::AgentId,
  ) -> Result<tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<AgentEvent>, Error>;

  fn subscribe_outbound(
      &self,
      agent_id: crate::history::AgentId,
  ) -> Result<tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver<AgentEvent>, Error>;

  fn send_inbound(
      &self,
      agent_id: crate::history::AgentId,
      block: crate::history::Block,
  ) -> Result<(), Error>;

  fn route(
      &self,
      from: crate::history::AgentId,
      to: crate::history::AgentId,
  ) -> Result<(), Error>;
  ```
  - `register`: called once per Agent at construction. Returns the broadcast sender it should publish to AND the mpsc receiver it should read inbound Blocks from. The bus retains a clone of the broadcast sender and the mpsc sender keyed by AgentId.
  - `outbound`: hands back a clone of an Agent's outbound broadcast sender (the Agent uses this internally; the Harness uses it to publish externally-originated `BlockComplete`s if needed — Phase 1 does not, but the seam exists).
  - `subscribe_outbound`: hands back a fresh broadcast `Receiver` for any subscriber.
  - `send_inbound`: pushes a `Block` (typically a `UserMessage` from the Harness) onto an Agent's inbound mpsc.
  - `route(from, to)`: spawns a task on the runtime that subscribes to `from`'s outbound, filters for `BlockComplete` carrying an `AgentMessage` block, and pushes each onto `to`'s inbound. Returns immediately. In Phase 1 this is unused (single Agent) but must compile and be tested.
- `pub struct InMemoryMessageBus { /* internal */ }`
  - `pub fn new() -> Self`
  - Implements `MessageBus`.
  - Storage: `std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<AgentId, AgentEntry>>` where `AgentEntry { outbound: broadcast::Sender<AgentEvent>, inbound: mpsc::Sender<Block> }`.
  - Broadcast channel capacity: `1024` (document with a one-line comment that it is lossy by design per plan.md §"Outbound channel"); mpsc capacity: `64` (deliberate; do not unbound).
  - All public methods take `&self` (no `&mut`).

## Tests
- `register_then_send_inbound_delivers_block`: register an agent, call `send_inbound` with a `UserMessage`, recv from the returned receiver, assert content.
- `subscribe_outbound_sees_published_events`: subscribe, then send a `TokenDelta` through the bus's outbound sender clone, assert the subscriber receives it.
- `route_forwards_block_complete_with_agent_message`: register two agents A and B, call `route(A, B)`, then publish a `BlockComplete { block: HistoryNode wrapping an AgentMessage }` through A's outbound sender; assert B's inbound receives the corresponding `Block::AgentMessage`. (Use `tokio::time::timeout` to bound the test at 1s; any failure to receive within that window is a test fail. Do not use sleeps.)
- `unknown_agent_returns_unknown_agent_error`: call `send_inbound` on a never-registered AgentId, assert `Err(Error::UnknownAgent(_))`.

## Wiring
- `src/lib.rs` already declares `pub mod messagebus;` (Step 1 work).
- No change to crate-root `Error` enum in this Bead.

## Definition of Done
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo nextest run` (incl. these new tests) pass.
- No STYLE_GUIDE.md violations.
- No use of `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.

## Out of scope
- Cross-process bus implementations
- Backpressure policies beyond fixed capacities
- Harness wiring (Step 6 owns the bus)

title: Phase 1 Step 6: Harness, User, lifecycle
description:
## Goal
Implement `User`/`UserId`/`Owner`, the `Harness` struct, `HarnessBuilder`, and the start/shutdown lifecycle. The Harness owns the `MessageBus`, the model registry, and the Agent registry. It exposes `send_to_agent` and `subscribe`.

This Bead does NOT implement the Agent event-loop task — that is Step 7. The Harness must compile and start without it; for now, the "Agent task" the Harness spawns may be a placeholder closure that simply selects on the cancellation token and a drain of the inbound receiver (so the lifecycle is testable end-to-end).

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §Harness, §Agent (authoritative)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 6: Harness, User, lifecycle"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`

## Implement in `src/user.rs`
- Do NOT define `UserId` here. Per bd-2ix, the canonical home for both `UserId` and `AgentId` is `src/history.rs` (they are tightly coupled to `Block` variants and define the data model). Re-export it from `src/user.rs`:
  ```
  pub use crate::history::UserId;
  ```
- `pub struct User { id: UserId }` — fields private.
- `impl User`:
  - `pub fn new() -> Self` — generates a random `UserId::new()`.
  - `pub fn id(&self) -> UserId`
- `impl Default for User { fn default() -> Self { Self::new() } }`

## Implement in `src/harness.rs`

### Dependency approval
**`tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["rt"] }` is APPROVED for this Bead.** Add it to `Cargo.toml`'s `[dependencies]` block. Use `tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken` for cooperative shutdown. Rationale: hand-rolling shutdown via `tokio::sync::Notify` has known foot-guns; `CancellationToken` is the canonical solution.

This is the ONLY new dependency authorized for Step 6.

### Types

**`ModelId`**
```
pub struct ModelId(String);
```
Derive `Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash`. Provide `pub fn new(name: impl Into<String>) -> Self`.

**`PendingAgent`** — the builder accumulates this struct, NOT a real `Agent` (the real `Agent` lands in Step 7). Define it here for now; Step 7 will replace it as needed.
```
pub struct PendingAgent {
    pub id: crate::history::AgentId,
    pub system_prompt: String,
    pub model_id: ModelId,
}
```
Public fields are acceptable here because this is a plain data carrier consumed by the builder. Derive `Clone, Debug`.

**`Harness`** — fields private:
- `models: HashMap<ModelId, Arc<dyn crate::model::ARModel>>`
- `agents: HashMap<crate::history::AgentId, AgentHandle>` where `AgentHandle` is a private struct holding the spawned task's `JoinHandle<Result<(), Error>>` AND the broadcast `Sender<AgentEvent>` clone (so `subscribe` can hand out fresh receivers without going through the bus every time — though going through the bus is fine too)
- `bus: Arc<crate::messagebus::InMemoryMessageBus>`
- `owner: crate::user::User`
- `cancel: tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken`
- `pending: Vec<PendingAgent>` — populated by `HarnessBuilder::build()`; consumed by `Harness::start()`

**`HarnessBuilder`**:
- `pub fn new() -> Self` — empty registries
- `pub fn add_model(self, id: ModelId, model: Arc<dyn ARModel>) -> Self`
- `pub fn add_agent(self, agent: PendingAgent) -> Self`
- `pub fn build(self) -> Result<Harness, Error>` — validates that every `PendingAgent::model_id` exists in `models`; returns `Err(Error::UnknownModel(id))` if not. Instantiates the `User` (the Owner) here.
- `impl Default for HarnessBuilder { fn default() -> Self { Self::new() } }`

### Lifecycle methods on `Harness`
- `pub fn owner_id(&self) -> crate::user::UserId`
- `pub fn start(&mut self) -> Result<(), Error>` — for each `PendingAgent`:
  1. Register it with the bus to obtain its outbound `Sender<AgentEvent>` and inbound `Receiver<Block>`.
  2. Spawn a `tokio::task` that is the **PLACEHOLDER** for the real Agent loop (Step 7 replaces this). The placeholder body:
     ```rust
     async move {
         loop {
             tokio::select! {
                 _ = cancel.cancelled() => return Ok::<(), Error>(()),
                 maybe = inbound.recv() => {
                     if maybe.is_none() { return Ok(()); }
                     // discard the block in Phase 1 Step 6 placeholder
                 }
             }
         }
     }
     ```
  3. Store the `JoinHandle` and the outbound `Sender` clone in `AgentHandle` keyed by the AgentId.

  `start` returns `Ok(())` once all spawns complete; it does NOT await them.

- `pub async fn run_until_shutdown(self) -> Result<(), Error>` — consumes `self`; awaits every spawned `JoinHandle` in turn. Returns the first error encountered (or `Ok(())` if all succeeded). Lifecycle shape: `start()` is synchronous; `run_until_shutdown()` is the awaitable hold-point. This is a justified deviation from plan.md's `start() -> JoinHandle`. Document with one terse comment.

- `pub fn shutdown(&self)` — `self.cancel.cancel()`. Idempotent (cancellation tokens are idempotent by design).

- `pub fn send_to_agent(&self, agent_id: AgentId, from: UserId, content: String) -> Result<(), Error>`:
  - Build `Block::UserMessage { from, content, timestamp: SystemTime::now() }`.
  - Forward to `bus.send_inbound(agent_id, block)`.
  - Map `messagebus::Error` via `From`.

- `pub fn subscribe(&self, agent_id: AgentId) -> Result<broadcast::Receiver<AgentEvent>, Error>`:
  - Proxy to `bus.subscribe_outbound(agent_id)`.

### `Error` enum
```
pub enum Error {
    UnknownModel(ModelId),
    UnknownAgent(crate::history::AgentId),
    Lifecycle(String),
    Bus(crate::messagebus::Error),
    Join(String),
}
```
Derive `Debug`. Manual `Display` + `std::error::Error`. `source()` returns `Some(&bus_err)` for `Bus`, `None` otherwise. `From<messagebus::Error> for Error` mapping to `Bus(...)`. `From<tokio::task::JoinError> for Error` mapping to `Join(err.to_string())`.

### Tests
- `builder_rejects_agent_with_unknown_model` — assert `Err(UnknownModel(_))`.
- `start_then_shutdown_exits_cleanly` — bound the test with `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), ...)`. Build a Harness with one Agent and a `StubArModel` (test-local). `start()`. `shutdown()`. `run_until_shutdown().await` returns `Ok(())`.
- `send_to_agent_routes_through_bus` — register an Agent. Start. Subscribe to ITS outbound (just to keep the broadcast alive — the placeholder loop discards inbound, so we can't directly observe inbound delivery via subscribe). Instead, expose `bus()` as `pub(crate)` ONLY for tests, OR construct a parallel `InMemoryMessageBus` reference for the test. Simplest: add a `#[cfg(test)] pub fn bus(&self) -> &Arc<InMemoryMessageBus>` accessor and use it from the test to register a second listener. Document this with one terse `#[cfg(test)]` comment.
- `subscribe_returns_receiver_seeing_published_event` — subscribe to an Agent's outbound, then publish a `TokenDelta` via the bus's outbound sender; subscriber receives it within `timeout(1s, ...)`.

`StubArModel` lives in the test module returning an empty `Stream` (use `futures::stream::empty()` boxed and pinned).

- No banner comments. No `unwrap`/`expect` outside tests. No `plan.md`/bead-ID references in source.
